name: bun
description: A fast bundler, transpiler, JavaScript Runtime and package manager for web software

subcommands.name: c
subcommands.description: Start a new project from a template

options.description: On finish, start bun and open in browser

args.name: template
args.description: Package from @bun-examples, GitHub repo, or local file

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Run a package.json script or executable

options.description: Always request the latest versions from the registry & reinstall all dependencies
Store & load cached data from a specific directory path
Platform-specific optimizations for installing dependencies. For macOS, 'clonefile' (default), 'copyfile'
Link 'bin' from a matching platform-specific 'optionalDependencies' instead. Default: esbuild, turbo
Skip verifying integrity of newly downloaded packages
Skip lifecycle scripts in the project's package.json (dependency scripts are never run)
Always request the latest versions from the registry & reinstall all dependencies
Store & load cached data from a specific directory path
Platform-specific optimizations for installing dependencies. For macOS, 'clonefile' (default), 'copyfile'
Link 'bin' from a matching platform-specific 'optionalDependencies' instead. Default: esbuild, turbo
Skip verifying integrity of newly downloaded packages
Skip lifecycle scripts in the project's package.json (dependency scripts are never run)
Always request the latest versions from the registry & reinstall all dependencies
Store & load cached data from a specific directory path
Platform-specific optimizations for installing dependencies. For macOS, 'clonefile' (default), 'copyfile'
Link 'bin' from a matching platform-specific 'optionalDependencies' instead. Default: esbuild, turbo
Skip verifying integrity of newly downloaded packages
Skip lifecycle scripts in the project's package.json (dependency scripts are never run)

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Bundle files using Bun's native bundler

options.description: Force a script or package to use Bun's runtime instead of Node.js (via symlinking node)
Changes the function called when compiling JSX elements using the classic JSX runtime
Changes the function called when compiling JSX fragments
Declares the module specifier to be used for importing the jsx and jsxs factory functions. Default: "react"
Print version with revision and exit
Use less memory, but run garbage collection more often
Disable macros from being executed in the bundler, transpiler and runtime
Activate Bun's debugger for a file
Activate Bun's Debugger, wait for a connection before executing
Activate Bun's Debugger, set breakpoint on first line of code and wait
Exit if the entrypoint does not exist
Enable auto reload in the Bun runtime, test runner, or bundler
Automatically restart the process on file change
Disable auto install in the Bun runtime
Automatically install dependencies and use global cache in Bun's runtime, equivalent to --install=fallback
Skip staleness checks for packages in the Bun runtime and resolve from disk
Use the latest matching versions of packages in the Bun runtime, always checking npm
Don't repeat the command for bun run
Generate a standalone Bun executable containing your bundled code
Specifies the module format to build to. Only esm is supported currently
Root directory used for multiple entry points
A prefix to be appended to any import paths in bundled code
Customize entry point filenames. Defaults to "[dir]/[name].[ext]"
Transpile file only, do not bundle
Dump environment variables from .env and process as JSON and quit. Useful for debugging
Dump system limits. Useful for debugging

args.name: entrypoints
args.description: Entrypoint to bundle. If multiple entrypoints provided, must specify --outdir

options.description: Always request the latest versions from the registry & reinstall all dependencies
Store & load cached data from a specific directory path
Platform-specific optimizations for installing dependencies. For macOS, 'clonefile' (default), 'copyfile'
Link 'bin' from a matching platform-specific 'optionalDependencies' instead. Default: esbuild, turbo
Skip verifying integrity of newly downloaded packages
Skip lifecycle scripts in the project's package.json (dependency scripts are never run)

subcommands.name: link
subcommands.description: Run without an argument to register this package to the global package registry. Uses the name field from package.json

args.name: package
args.description: Install a package from the global package registry

subcommands.name: unlink
subcommands.description: Unlink this package from the global package registry

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Get the latest version of bun

options.description: Force a script or package to use Bun's runtime instead of Node.js (via symlinking node)
Changes the function called when compiling JSX elements using the classic JSX runtime
Changes the function called when compiling JSX fragments
Declares the module specifier to be used for importing the jsx and jsxs factory functions. Default: "react"
Print version with revision and exit
Use less memory, but run garbage collection more often
Disable macros from being executed in the bundler, transpiler and runtime
Activate Bun's debugger for a file

Don't repeat the command for bun run
Set the per-test timeout in milliseconds, default is 5000
Re-run each test file <NUMBER> times, helps catch certain bugs
Only run tests that are marked with "test.only()"
Include tests that are marked with "test.todo()"
Exit the test suite after <NUMBER> failures. If you do not specify a number, it defaults to 1
Run only tests with a name that matches the given regex

Dump system limits. Useful for debugging

subcommands.name: pm
subcommands.description: Set of utilities for working with Bun's package manager

options.description: Print the path to bin folder
Print the path to the cache folder
Generate & print the hash of the current lockfile
Print the hash stored in the current lockfile
Print the string used to hash the lockfile
List the dependency tree according to the current lockfile

subcommands.name: repl
subcommands.description: Run a REPL (read eval print loop) with the Bun runtime.(experimental)
